_source: Extracted from the web build's brickell-city.html (task A2). Coordinates converted from that file's Three.js source units (approximately meters, matching its own '1KM DEMO' scale hint) to UE centimeters via a x100 scale factor.
_note: This is a rebuild, not a port (see CLAUDE.md and docs/UE5_SHOWPIECE_PLAN.md section 0): only the seven real named landmarks and the fixed road/water grid are extracted here, since those are deterministic in the source. brickell-city.html also procedurally scatters generic filler buildings across the block grid using Math.random() at load time, so there is no fixed list of those to extract faithfully; task B2 (Megascans + art pass) fills the blocks with Fab/Megascans assets by eye instead, using this road grid and these landmarks as the anchors.
_water_note: The source has two water bodies (a bay and a river). This schema's 'water' field is a single bay polygon per task A2's spec; the smaller river channel along z<-25000 is not encoded here.

buildings.note: Open-air luxury mall and towers, the anchor of Brickell (701 S Miami Ave).
Secondary tower in the City Centre complex.
Secondary tower in the City Centre complex.
Low-rise dining and nightlife block just west (901 S Miami Ave).
One of the tallest towers on Brickell Avenue, south end (1435 Brickell Ave).
S Miami Avenue tower (1300 S Miami Ave).
Metrorail transit hub on the southwest edge.

landmarks_without_geometry.note: Rare hammock green space, the map's only park. Ground plane only in the source, no building mass; add Fab foliage in task B2.
Drawbridge crossing the Miami River into downtown. Bespoke deck/pillar geometry in the source, not a simple box; model separately in task B2.

water.note: Biscayne Bay, east of the WATER_X boundary in the source. The Miami River (a second, smaller water body along the south edge) is not included; see _water_note.
